meaning: adj.有关植物生长的,植物的,有生长力的,生活呆板单调
examples: She was in what was described as a vegetative state.
She had been in what doctors described as an irreversible vegetative state for five years.
That is when it's not covered in snow and there is no frost covering the grass and vegetative parts of a plant's herbs and the flowers the marmots like to eat.
Only 7% of people who enter into a prolonged vegetative state have good recoveries.
The odds of recovery drop to almost zero after a year of being vegetative.
